title: The History of Don Quixote, Volume 2, Part 38
summaries: "The History of Don Quixote, Volume 2, Part 38" by Miguel de Cervantes Saavedra is a section of a novel originally published in two parts in 1605 and 1615. This installment continues the adventures of an aging hidalgo who has lost his mind from reading too many chivalric romances and believes himself a knight-errant. Alongside his earthy squire Sancho Panza, Don Quixote navigates a world where illusion and reality blur, pursuing his chivalric fantasies with comic and tragic consequences in this foundational work of Western literature. (This is an automatically generated summary.)
subjects: Spain -- Social life and customs -- 16th century -- Fiction
